---
title: "Stable Sphere Calculus"
---

Stable sphere calculus studies what remains orderly when suspension is repeated. In this repository, the current layer is finite: it records repeatable suspension-count facts before attempting any full stable homotopy theory.

::: {.warning-box}
This page teaches the finite seed only. It does not claim a completed stable homotopy or spectral sequence theory.
:::

## What To Notice

- Repeated suspension is treated as a computable finite operation.
- The checked seed gives a safe pattern for double and fourfold suspension facts.
- The long-term stable-sphere language must stay downstream of theorem manifests.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

Why is it useful to prove finite repeated-suspension facts before trying to talk about stable spheres in general?

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2S-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2S-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2S-T0003"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2S-T0004"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2S-T0005"></div>

<p data-paper-ref="papers/phase2/PAPER_P2_01_STABLE_SPHERE_CALCULUS.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/phase2/PAPER_P2_01_STABLE_SPHERE_CALCULUS.md">Stable Sphere Calculus</a></p>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
